column11: Please provide any text written feedback you may have from your session.

This is really impressive. It would be a great help for historians working within the VRTI. It has a lot of potential, particularly after historians work with it extensively and identify false positives etc., while also providing a good way of identifying people not on the KG so it can guide future work.
Quick intro or explanatory text wld be good for first time user.
Please consider automating even further place entity matches with the VRTI links by capturing whethet the level of the place in the hierarchy (a province/county/barony/parish/townland).
System with great potential - very straightforward to use.

The system was well designed and easy to understand. I found the process of uploading a document and identifying candidates from the knowledge graph very straightforward. The color-coded status for each candidate was particularly helpful, making it easy to quickly assess their relevance. The explanatory text outlining why each candidate was suggested added valuable context and transparency to the results. Additionally, the ability to export the matches in various formats would be useful for storing and sharing the results.
I think this is a clear approach to identifying and verifying entities within the VRTI and would welcome the chance to use it

Reasoning regarding last question: it's a perfect tool for its intended audience (historians / NER researchers), but as a general tool would require much more explanation to someone unfamiliar with the field. (Only flagging this as the questions here are very general, and my answers would be very different if it was intended for a general audience haha). Reasoning regarding the "support of a technical person" question, I put "neutral" because, while the tool is easy and intuitive to use, I think a tutorial such as provided during the call was very helpful, and I can imagine a situation where I just open the tool with nobody to instruct me I would take a bit longer to figure out how it works and might miss certain features. If this was a final product where the creator wasn't available to quickly explain the tool to every user, I'd suggest maybe adding a quick overview tutorial or like pop-up information screen to guide a first-time user into how to use the app (if this is already present and I missed it, apologies!)
